purpose: This file tracks external App Store gates that cannot be proven by local build/tests alone. Set an item to true only after the listed evidence exists.

appleDeveloper.evidence: Apple Developer > Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles > App ID shows Sign in with Apple enabled.

firebaseAuth.evidence: Firebase Authentication > Sign-in method > Apple is enabled and configured; Apple service identifier return URL matches Firebase's Apple callback URL.

hosting.evidence: Firebase Hosting deploy completed on 2026-07-01 after the accessibility URL addition; npm run preflight:ios:live passed against https://ucla-knee-mri.firebaseapp.com, including public privacy/support/accessibility/account routes, lazy-loaded route chunks, account deletion UI, Sign in with Apple, App Review demo, the native iOS shell marker, and both Firebase Auth handler endpoints.

archiveExport.evidence: npm run archive:ios:signing shows App Store export signing ready: yes, and npm run export:ios or Xcode Organizer can access App Store Connect for Team X578T4K65B. Keep false until the App Store distribution provisioning profile exists and export/upload no longer fails with account access.

realDeviceAuth.evidence: Real iPhone/TestFlight build signs in through Google, signs in through Apple, and enters App Review demo.

accountDeletion.evidence: The app has an in-app request path, deployed Firestore rules for accountDeletionRequests, and scripts/process-account-deletion.mjs for admin fulfillment. Keep false until a signed-in test user submits a request and the admin dry-run/fulfillment process is verified.

screenshots.evidence: iPhone 6.9-inch and iPad 13-inch App Store screenshot sets captured from native iOS simulator Debug build on 2026-07-01; npm run screenshots:ios:check passed 32 checks; npm run screenshots:ios:evidence:verify passed 3/3 gates; Codex visual review found no PHI, real learner data, login screen, localhost/browser chrome, simulator notification banner, or debug overlay.

appStoreConnect.evidence: App Store Connect version 1.0 has metadata, privacy, age rating, regulated medical device status, screenshots, selected TestFlight build, and review notes. Keep submittedForReview false until after the final App Review submission action is complete.
